title: STANDARD FOR UNSHELLED PISTACHIO NUTS CXS 131-1981 Adopted in 1981. Amended in 2022, 2023.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR UNSHELLED PISTACHIO NUTS CXS 131-1981 Adopted in 1981. Amended in 2022, 2023.
sections.summary: Title of the Codex standard: "Standard for Unshelled Pistachio Nuts (CXS 131-1981)", adopted 1981 and amended in 2022 and 2023.
2022 amendment note: following CAC decisions, Section 7.2 on labelling of non‑retail containers was amended.
2023 amendment note: following CAC decisions, wording in Section 8 on methods of analysis and sampling was amended.
Scope: this standard covers unshelled Pistacia vera L. pistachios, natural or processed, intended for direct consumption or packed in bulk for repacking into consumer containers.
Description section outlining the product definition, varietal types (long, round), presentation styles (raw, roasted), substyles (salted, lime‑juice treated) and optional size classification.
Definition: pistachios are mature seeds of Pistacia vera L. that have been artificially sun‑dried and naturally or mechanically opened, and may be roasted, salted and/or lime‑juice treated.
Varietal classification: pistachios are classified as Long pistachio or Round pistachio.
Presentation styles: the product shall be presented as either Raw pistachio or Roasted pistachio.
Substyles: the product may be presented as Salted and/or Lime‑juice treated.
Optional size classification by number of pistachios per 100 g: Small >106; Medium 92–106; Large 81–91; Very Large 71–80; Extra Large <71.

Composition and quality requirements: final product moisture ≤7% m/m and pistachios must be practically free from mould/rancid taste, free from live insects/mites and practically free from foreign matter; defects (closedness, emptiness, unripeness, insect damage, mould) have maximum allowances (5%, 5%, 8%, 4%, 1% respectively) with total defects ≤10%; lot acceptance requires no live infestation and subsamples meeting moisture and quality criteria per an AQL 6.5 sampling plan.
Requires clean, sound pistachios of a quality suitable for human consumption.
Permits only salt and lime-juice as optional ingredients.

Specifies final-product requirements for unshelled pistachios including a maximum moisture of 7% m/m, general quality criteria (mould/insect/foreign-matter limits), definitions of specific defects, numeric allowances for those defects, and a total-defect limit.
Sets maximum moisture content for the product at 7 percent m/m.

Final product must be practically free from mould and rancid taste, free from living insects and mites, and practically free from foreign matter (anything other than pistachio kernel, hard shell and pericarp).

Defines defects for pistachios: (a) closedness (unsplit shells with developed kernel), (b) emptiness (undeveloped kernel), (c) unripeness/immaturity (inadequately developed kernel), (d) insect-damaged fruit (including dead insects/mites), and (e) mouldy fruit (visible mould or decay).
Allocates maximum defect allowances by count: closedness 5%, emptiness 5%, unripeness 8%, insect damage 4%, mouldy fruit 1%, with total defects (a–e) not to exceed 10%.

A lot is acceptable if there is no evidence of live infestation and subsamples taken per an appropriate sampling plan (AQL 6.5) meet the requirements of 3.3.1 and 3.3.2 and do not exceed the defect allowances in 3.3.4.
No food additives are permitted in the product.

Recommends preparation and handling per the General Principles of Food Hygiene and requires the product, as far as good manufacturing practice allows, to be free from objectionable matter and—when tested—free from microorganisms, parasites, or microbial-origin substances in amounts that may represent a health hazard.
Containers shall be as full as practicable without impairing quality and must be consistent with a proper declaration of contents.

States that specific labelling provisions supplement the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods, covering the product name/presentation requirements (7.1) and labelling of non-retail containers (7.2).

Requires the label name to be “unshelled pistachio”, “unshelled pistachio nuts” or “inshell pistachio nuts”, to indicate presentation form (raw or roasted), and allows inclusion of varietal type ("long"/"round"), substyle ("salted"/"lime-juice treated"), and size designation ("small" to "extra large").
Advises that labelling of non-retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Specifies that methods for checking compliance with the standard shall be those in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions.
